title: Capacity architecture & activation options for QuantumBridge
preview:
From: David Park <david.park@quantumbridge.ai>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
Cc: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@quantumbridge.ai>, procurement@quantumbridge.ai
Date: 2026-08-22T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Re: sizing discussion follow-up

Alex — thanks again for the demo this morning. QuantumBridge is excited about the latency profile and the KV cache savings you showed. Before we move to legal/procurement, procurement asked for a clear set of options for committed capacity: a) a conservative minimum-guarantee (to match our predictable inference peaks), b) a phased ramp (3-month onramp plan), and c) an aggressive committed+burst plan for Q4. Can you send a short doc/worksheet with cluster sizes, minimum GPU floors, expected p99 latency at typical batch sizes, and rough monthly committed cost for each option? Would also like to understand fallback behavior if dedicated capacity is saturated. —David

Attachments: qb-initial-load-patterns.csv (stub)

From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
To: David Park <david.park@quantumbridge.ai>, Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@quantumbridge.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-22T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: sizing discussion follow-up

David — good request. I'm attaching a short worksheet that lays out three architecture options and the activation timelines. High level: 1) Baseline (min-guarantee): 8-GPU lane (A40/80 equiv) with autoscaling to 16 for bursts; target p99 ~220ms for 1k-token avg prompts when batching is enabled; monthly committed cost estimate $62k. 2) Phased Ramp: start at 4 GPUs for month 1, add 8 GPUs month 2, reach 16 by month 3; this lowers initial spend and lets us tune batching/quantization thresholds; effective committed floor when fully ramped: 16 GPUs; average p99 180–200ms at higher batch efficiency. 3) Committed+Burst: 24 reserved GPUs with a separate burst pool (ephemeral) up to 40 GPUs for short windows — suitable if you have Q4 spikes. Bursting behavior: when dedicated pool is saturated, we can route low-priority traffic to a compatible hosted instance (warm standby model) or soft-throttle/queue with SLA-grade notifications.

I've summarized assumptions inside the sheet (attached). Two asks for you: confirm target p99 budget (what latency SLA matters for user-facing inference vs async tasks), and share an expected token distribution (short/med/long) so we can refine kernel selection and the lane sizing.

I copied Ben to provide commercial numbers and Laura for activation sequencing.

Attachments: qb-capacity-options-v1.xlsx (spreadsheet)

—Alex Martinez
Senior Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: David Park <david.park@quantumbridge.ai>
Cc: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-23T08:27:00-07:00
Subject: Re: sizing discussion follow-up

David — quick commercial note to align expectations. The spreadsheet shows committed monthly pricing per GPU-equivalent and a blended discount based on 12-month vs 36-month term. For the Baseline (8-GPU floor) the monthly committed price is $62k as Alex noted (includes reserved compute + baseline support). Phased Ramp (4 -> 8 -> 16) carries a pro-rated ramp credit in months 1–3; fully ramped committed spend is ~ $112k/mo. Committed+Burst is priced at $160k/mo committed with burst capacity billed separately at usage rates; we can include a capped burst budget for predictability.

If procurement wants a safety clause: suggest adding an automatic capacity review at 90 days and a one-time adjust within the first 120 days to realign floors with actual traffic (min 30-day notice before reductions). Happy to run a short TCO comparison vs hosted-only for your expected workload if you share the token profile.

—Ben
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

From: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@quantumbridge.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@quantumbridge.ai, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-24T10:10:00-07:00
Subject: Re: capacity architecture & activation options for QuantumBridge

Thanks Alex and Ben — the worksheet helps. A couple of follow-ups from our end: 1) Can you spell out how autoscaling interacts with committed floors? For example: if we reserve an 8-GPU lane and autoscaling is enabled to 16, what portion of that autoscale is guaranteed vs burst (and how is it billed)? 2) For the phased ramp option, procurement would like explicit activation milestones and acceptance criteria (e.g., performance verification on their eval prompts, key rotation and audit logs in place). 3) We need a fallback SLO: if dedicated lane falls below p99 SLA because of saturation, what is the typical fallback sequence and expected user impact windows?

Also looping Rishi from Redwood for technical confirmation on kernel/quantization compatibility and to confirm whether we can meet encryption & KMS requirements during activation.

—Elena
Head of Platform Engineering, QuantumBridge

From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
To: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@quantumbridge.ai>, Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-25T16:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: capacity architecture & activation options for QuantumBridge

Elena — quick technical notes to address your points: 1) Autoscaling vs guaranteed floor: reserved lane (8 GPUs) is the guaranteed capacity; autoscaling pool is configured as part of the deployment policy. We support two autoscale bands: a reserved-anchored autoscale (where additional instances are still billed under the committed contract at a discounted overage rate) and a purely ephemeral burst (billed at on-demand rates). For your procurement template we can set the reserved-anchored autoscale to up to +100% (so 8 -> 16) with an overage discount tier (e.g., 40% off on-demand) for predictable cost. 2) Activation milestones for phased ramp: (a) Week 0–2: provisioning and baseline tests (connectivity, KMS, audit log flow); (b) Week 3–6: 4 GPU lane active, ingest test traffic, run evaluation set; (c) Week 7–10: expand to 8 GPUs, validate latency/p99 with production sampling; (d) Week 11–14: expand to 16 GPUs, finalize canary rollout and retention policies. Each milestone ends with a short sign-off checklist (performance targets, security checklist, SLO smoke tests). 3) Fallback sequence: first, we direct low-priority or async requests to the hosted fallback model variant (same model family, slightly higher latency and lower batching efficiency). Second, if that is insufficient, we apply short-lived queueing with progressive retry—this preserves high-priority user flows. Typical user impact window for fallback events is 30–120 seconds to transition and warm the fallback; we include real-time alerts and a traffic-split rollback if degradation exceeds configurable thresholds.

I attached a small archive with example eval traces and a minimal activation checklist. Happy to sync on a 30-min call to walk procurement through the milestone language if helpful.

Attachments: quantumbridge-eval-traces.zip (archive, stub)

—Rishi Malhotra
Director, Serving Runtime, Redwood Inference

title: PO issuance needed — onboarding ledger gap & spend cap question
preview:
From: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@blueharbor.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 24 Aug 2026 17:04:00 -0700
Subject: Vendor onboarding — PO issuance and requested spend cap for pilot

Hi Ben,

Thanks again — attached is our completed onboarding form (BlueHarbor_Onboarding_Form.pdf). We are ready to kick off a 3-month pilot next week. To proceed with procurement on our side I need a Redwood PO number so our AP team can map costs and release the initial invoice.

Requested spend cap: $95,000 (estimate for compute + support blocks). Suggested line item split:
- compute-hours: $80,000
- support-blocks (SLA/phone): $15,000

One wrinkle: our internal ledger code is BH-TECH-01 but our legal / PO system needs Redwood to supply a PO number and indicate your ledger/cost center. Can you confirm which cost center we should map the PO to on your side? If you can issue a provisional PO number (or confirm what the final PO will be) that’ll get our AP to release initial payment.

Please cc procurement/finance as needed.

Thanks,
Elena Ruiz
Vendor Ops — Blue Harbor Logistics
elena.ruiz@blueharbor.com
+1 415 555 0123

Attachment: BlueHarbor_Onboarding_Form.pdf (PDF, 1 page)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@blueharbor.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 25 Aug 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Vendor onboarding — PO issuance and requested spend cap for pilot

Elena — thanks for the form, received and uploaded.

Quick clarifying Qs before I route this to Finance for PO issuance:
- Confirm billing entity: Blue Harbor Logistics LLC (EIN/TIN)?
- Do you need the PO to reference BH-TECH-01 as the customer ledger, or do you want Redwood to put our cost center on the PO (we can add a mapping note) — I ask because our finance group prefers internal cost center ENG-LLM-01 for pilot spend.

Next steps from my side: I will open a spendcap override request and ask Finance to issue a provisional PO so your AP can match the invoice. I attached our quick onboarding checklist for any missing fields. If you can confirm the billing entity + tax id I’ll push for an expedited turnaround.

Thanks — Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | (650) 555-0199

Attachment: redwood_vendor_onboarding_checklist.xlsx (XLSX, 1 sheet)

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 25 Aug 2026 11:23:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Vendor onboarding — PO issuance and requested spend cap for pilot

Laura — forwarding Elena's onboarding; Ben flagged the ledger mapping question. TL;DR: Blue Harbor is asking us to include their ledger BH-TECH-01 on the PO so their AP can post correctly. Our standard is to put internal cost center ENG-LLM-01.

Request: please create a provisional PO (provisional is fine for 1st invoice) with an initial spend cap of $100,000 (a small buffer over their ask) and two line items:
1) Compute-hours — $82,500 — GL: ENG-LLM-01
2) Support-blocks — $17,500 — GL: ENG-OPS-230

Reason: avoids immediate spend blocking on their side and gives us wiggle room for early usage spikes. If Finance is uncomfortable with $100k, the team is fine with a final of ~$98.5k. I attached a one-page spendcap override justification.

Can you advise expected PO number and whether we can add a "Customer ledger" free-text field with BH-TECH-01 so Blue Harbor's AP can map the incoming invoice?

Thanks — Karthik

Attachment: spendcap_override_request.docx (DOCX, 1 page)

- Total spend cap on PO: $98,500 (finalized after internal approval)
- Line items:
  * 0001 Compute-hours — $81,000 — Cost center: ENG-LLM-01
  * 0002 Support-blocks — $17,500 — Cost center: ENG-OPS-230

Notes: We adjusted compute estimate slightly to balance rounding. If you need a hard cap at exactly $95,000 we can re-run approvals, but this PO is approved for $98,500 so invoices up to that amount will be accepted against PO-2026-4598.

Please confirm receipt and acceptance of the PO. If accepted, we'll mark onboarding complete on our side and provide initial invoice instructions.

Laura Bennett

Redwood Inference

title: Request: temporary version lock + automatic cutover policy to meet SLOs
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Lee <jordan.lee@acme.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 22 Aug 2026 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: Request: temporary version lock + automatic cutover policy to meet SLOs

Jordan — thanks again for the sync this morning. Quick recap and a proposal to avoid the repeated 200–300ms latency blips your team saw yesterday during the canary traffic shifts.

Problem: when we push the v2.3 quantized build through the standard canary (5% -> 20% -> 50%), a subset of hosts hit a pathological kernel scheduling path and we observed 2–7 minute windows where p95 token latency spiked above your 500ms SLO. That’s transient but visible.

Proposal (what Redwood can do immediately):
- Apply a temporary "version lock" on Acme's production route: keep the currently pinned stable model (v2.2) as the default for live traffic for up to 72 hours while we run a controlled A/B cutover.
- Run an automated A/B canary in a confined cohort (internal/observability traffic only) with aggressive metric thresholds. If p95 latency for the cohort exceeds 20% over baseline for >3 consecutive minutes we auto-cut back to pinned v2.2.
- Enable automatic fallback: commit to immediate rollback/planned cutover paths (no manual gating) so your app never sees elevated latencies.

Attached are: proposed_rollout_policy.pdf (process + thresholds) and slo-impact-grid.xlsx (SLO simmer simulation for several failure modes).

Ask: Can Acme confirm it’s acceptable for Redwood to: 1) hold the stable model in production for 48–72 hours, and 2) accept our automatic cutover/fallback behavior as an SLA mitigation for this window? If yes, we’ll route a short amendment to your procurement/legal and trigger the policy in the control plane.

I’m looping Lauren on approvals and Hannah/Kevin in infra for telemetry; will stand up a dedicated Slack channel if that helps.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0127

Attachments: proposed_rollout_policy.pdf (16 pages), slo-impact-grid.xlsx (1 sheet)


From: Jordan Lee <jordan.lee@acme.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 22 Aug 2026 11:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Request: temporary version lock + automatic cutover policy to meet SLOs

Ben — thanks for the clear write-up and the attachments. A few quick clarifications before I loop procurement: 

- Does the auto-cutback affect session affinity or KV cache warmness for our live users? We saw some tokenization regressions in the last fallback.
- Who owns the decision to trigger a manual rollback if our app team wants to expedite? We prefer the ability to force a rollback from our side as well.
- Are there any cost impacts we should expect when traffic shifts to the pinned pool?

If those are acceptable, we are fine with the temporary version lock for 48 hours to stop user-facing blips. Procurement will need a short written amendment defining the exact SLO waiver window and the rollback authority.

Jordan Lee
Head of Platform Engineering, Acme Corp
jordan.lee@acme.com

On Sat, Aug 22, 2026 at 09:15 AM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com> wrote:
> Jordan — thanks again for the sync this morning. Quick recap and a proposal to avoid the repeated 200–300ms latency blips...


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 23 Aug 2026 08:42:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Request: temporary version lock + automatic cutover policy to meet SLOs

Internal — forwarding Acme's reply. Short ask: we need an expedited operational exception for Acme's route and a clear documented rollback authority. My notes below:

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Lee <jordan.lee@acme.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 22 Aug 2026 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: Request: temporary version lock + automatic cutover policy to meet SLOs

Jordan — thanks again for the sync this morning. Quick recap and a proposal to avoid the repeated 200–300ms latency blips your team saw yesterday during the canary traffic shifts.

Problem: when we push the v2.3 quantized build through the standard canary (5% -> 20% -> 50%), a subset of hosts hit a pathological kernel scheduling path and we observed 2–7 minute windows where p95 token latency spiked above your 500ms SLO. That’s transient but visible.

Proposal (what Redwood can do immediately):
- Apply a temporary "version lock" on Acme's production route: keep the currently pinned stable model (v2.2) as the default for live traffic for up to 72 hours while we run a controlled A/B cutover.
- Run an automated A/B canary in a confined cohort (internal/observability traffic only) with aggressive metric thresholds. If p95 latency for the cohort exceeds 20% over baseline for >3 consecutive minutes we auto-cut back to pinned v2.2.
- Enable automatic fallback: commit to immediate rollback/planned cutover paths (no manual gating) so your app never sees elevated latencies.

Attached are: proposed_rollout_policy.pdf (process + thresholds) and slo-impact-grid.xlsx (SLO simmer simulation for several failure modes).

Ask: Can Acme confirm it’s acceptable for Redwood to: 1) hold the stable model in production for 48–72 hours, and 2) accept our automatic cutover/fallback behavior as an SLA mitigation for this window? If yes, we’ll route a short amendment to your procurement/legal and trigger the policy in the control plane.

I’m looping Lauren on approvals and Hannah/Kevin in infra for telemetry; will stand up a dedicated Slack channel if that helps.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0127

Attachments: proposed_rollout_policy.pdf (16 pages), slo-impact-grid.xlsx (1 sheet)

If those are acceptable, we are fine with the temporary version lock for 48 hours to stop user-facing blips. Procurement will need a short written amendment defining the exact SLO waiver window and the rollback authority.

title: Novabio Systems - renewal amendment: tier rebalancing & bridge credit proposal
preview:
From: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@novabio.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>
Cc: Sasha Reynolds <sasha.reynolds@novabio.com>, procurement@novabio.com
Date: 2026-08-10T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Renewal question + possible capacity increase

Ben — thanks for the renewal notice. We're generally happy with Redwood's performance, but two items are blockers on our side: 1) the proposed list price increase, and 2) our projected usage for H2 has jumped (we expect ~2.2x current commit).

We need a path that gives us: a short-term price hold for Q4 pricing, and either a larger committed discount if we lock to a 12‑month tier now, or a temporary bridge credit while we ramp. Can you send options (discount tiers, one-time credits, and any flexibility on true-up cadence)?

Also looping Sasha (engineering lead) and procurement.

Thanks — Liam
Liam O'Connor
VP Product, NovaBio Systems


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>
To: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@novabio.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.inference>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.inference>
Date: 2026-08-11T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Renewal question + possible capacity increase

Liam — really appreciate the context and the heads-up on H2 volume. Short answer: yes, we can do structured options that combine a temporary pricehold + a bridge credit while you ramp, or a steeper committed discount if you lock a multi-quarter commit today.

Quick options to consider (high level):
- Option A (Ramp Bridge): 3‑month pricehold at current effective rate + one-time bridge credit sized to cover expected ramp delta, monthly true-up.
- Option B (Commit Upsell): 12‑month committed uplift (2.0x baseline) for a 22% blended discount vs list + quarterly true-up and volume step-down protection.
- Option C (Hybrid): 6‑month pricehold + reduced bridge credit + staged commit that converts to Option B after Q3 metrics.

I've attached a draft amendment that sketches Option A/B (Novabio_Renewal_Amendment_Draft_v1.pdf) and a short usage runbook that Sasha might find useful.

If it's helpful, I can schedule a 30m call tomorrow to walk through the numbers with you and Marissa. Which option looks closest to what procurement wants?

—Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference


From: Sasha Reynolds <sasha.reynolds@novabio.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.inference>
Cc: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@novabio.com>, procurement@novabio.com
Date: 2026-08-12T08:45:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Renewal question + possible capacity increase

Ben/Marissa — quick technical note: our usage profile is spiky during model training windows; sustained peak token rate matters for the GPU pool sizing. If Option B requires reserved capacity we need the ability to burst across zones for one month each quarter. Can you confirm whether the proposed committed uplift supports cross-zone bursts or if that triggers additional capacity fees?

Also, the team prefers monthly true-ups rather than quarterly for visibility.

—Sasha
Sasha Reynolds
Senior Engineering Manager, NovaBio Systems


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.inference>
To: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@novabio.com>, Sasha Reynolds <sasha.reynolds@novabio.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.inference>
Date: 2026-08-18T09:20:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Renewal question + possible capacity increase

Liam/Sasha — thanks for the clarity; couple of firm answers from our side:
1) Cross-zone bursts: supported under the Dedicated uplift, we can add a burst pool allowance for up to 30% of committed capacity for two discrete months per quarter without extra fees (we'll capture in amendment language).
2) True-up cadence: monthly is fine; we'll amend the billing schedule.
3) Bridge credit sizing: based on your stated 2.2x expectation, we propose a one-time bridge credit equal to 30% of the incremental committed spend expected during Q3 ramp under Option A. That keeps your short-term cash flow neutral while we confirm the uplift.

Attached is a revised amendment excerpt with explicit burst allowance and monthly true-up language. If you can confirm acceptance of the bridge sizing (or provide a different ramp estimate), we'll prepare a DocuSign-ready amendment and a proposed payment schedule.

Best — Marissa
Marissa Cole
Senior Customer Success Manager, Redwood Inference


From: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@novabio.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.inference>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.inference>, procurement@novabio.com
Date: 2026-08-24T16:40:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Renewal question + possible capacity increase

Marissa/Ben — thanks, that addresses the engineering ask. Procurement wants confirmation of two items before routing for signature:
- final amendment with the 30% bridge credit clause called out in the financial schedule, and a short clause that the bridge credit is applied as an invoice credit on the next billing cycle (not a separate refund),
- a 45‑day window to review any reserved-capacity addendum that would convert the bridge into a committed uplift (to allow our CFO time to sign off).

If you can send a DocuSign link with those two points included, we'll route internally and aim to have a signature decision within 7 business days.

Link to procurement PO template: procurement@novabio.com provided wiring instructions.

Liam


--- 
Notes: DocuSign draft to follow (Marissa). Internal next step: Marissa to produce amendment with explicit bridge credit application to invoice and 45-day option to convert. Add Monica on billing ops for invoice credit operations. HubSpot deal updated: NB-2026-RNW-0423.

title: Q4 renewal: transition Horizon Health invoices to AWS Marketplace billing
preview:
From: Avery Cole <avery.cole@horizonhealth.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: procurement@horizonhealth.com
Date: Fri, 21 Aug 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Horizon renewal & marketplace billing option

Hi Camila,

Hope you’re well — Horizon’s renewal is coming up (contract ends 2026-10-01) and our procurement team is pushing us to consolidate software spend through AWS Marketplace for easier PO and invoicing. Before we commit, we need to confirm a few items:

- Can Redwood accept renewals via AWS Marketplace so invoices hit our marketplace billing pipeline?
- Will pricing and SKUs be identical to our current direct contract, or is there a marketplace uplift/fee?
- What does the amendment process look like (DocuSign link, SOW changes)?
- Any required entitlement or marketplace subscription steps on our side?

I’m attaching the renewal RFP and our procurement checklist for reference.

We’d like to make a decision by 2026-09-15 to align internal approvals. Can we schedule a short call next week to outline the migration path?

Thanks,
Avery
Procurement Manager
Horizon Health
Attachment: Horizon_Renewal_RFP.pdf


From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Avery Cole <avery.cole@horizonhealth.com>
Cc: John Mills <john.mills@horizonhealth.com>, procurement@horizonhealth.com
Date: Fri, 21 Aug 2026 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Horizon renewal & marketplace billing option

Hi Avery — thanks for the note and the RFP. We can absolutely migrate your renewal to AWS Marketplace billing; happy to outline the steps and what Horizon will need to do. High level:

1) Confirm target renewal term and the SKU(s) you want on Marketplace.
2) We map your current seat/usage tiers to Marketplace SKUs and produce an invoice mapping CSV.
3) Redwood issues an amendment and DocuSign flow; Marketplace listing is used to route billing.
4) Finance verifies PO/entitlement and we cut over at renewal date.

Two quick clarifications: we typically keep list prices parity but Marketplace fees can cause a small delta — we can propose a one-time credit to smooth the transition if needed. Also, some customers prefer a 1-month overlap window to validate invoicing.

I’ve attached a short migration outline and proposed mutual action plan. Can you do Wed 8/26 at 10am PT for a 30-minute kickoff? If that works I’ll include Ben (pricing) and Steph (billing ops/legal) to join.

Best,
Camila Reyes
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference
Attachment: marketplace-migration-outline.pdf


From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 21 Aug 2026 11:18:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Horizon renewal & marketplace billing option — action items

Folks — forwarding Horizon’s request (below). Key asks: migrate renewal to AWS Marketplace, confirm SKU parity, and surface any uplift or one-time credits we can offer to close.

Proposed internal owners:
- Ben: pricing confirmation, SKU mapping, propose credit if needed
- Steph: billing/ops & legal for amendment + DocuSign flow + lead time on marketplace activation

Action items: please respond with the following by EOD Mon 8/24 so I can get back to procurement ahead of the kickoff:
1) Draft SKU mapping and any price differences (Ben)
2) Template amendment + estimated DocuSign turnaround (Steph)
3) Recommended go-live/cutover plan (30d/60d overlap) and any accounting impacts (both)

Thanks — I scheduled a tentative 8/26 10am PT slot with Horizon. Confirm availability and I’ll send calendar invite.

- Timing: if Horizon signs amendment by 9/15, marketplace activation and entitlement sync can be completed by 9/30.

On Mon, 24 Aug 2026 at 08:40 Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com> wrote:

> From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
> To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>

> Date: Mon, 24 Aug 2026 10:15:00 -0700
> Subject: RE: Subprocessors — plain-language explainer for procurement review>
>
> Hi everyone — thanks for the context, Ben. I reviewed the subprocessor list. Short answers up front:
>
> - We use a set of cloud infra providers (compute and storage), a managed observability vendor for system telemetry, a partner for model weights hosting in certain regions, and a (vendor) for billing/usage reporting.
> - Only our model-weights hosting partner and the compute/storage provider may process customer request/response content in rare situations (debug captures, region-specific hosting) — otherwise, telemetry and logs are limited to metadata and anonymized traces.
> - We maintain contractual controls (DPAs, SCCs/Known safeguards as appropriate) and encryption-at-rest/in-transit.
>
> Proposed 115-word blurb for Procurement (plain language):
>
> \"Redwood operates our hosted API using a small set of service providers for compute, storage, observability, and model hosting. Some partners may process customer requests in order to run or host models (for example, where models are hosted in a specific region or when debug captures are required to investigate issues). We use contractual safeguards and standard cross-border protections and encrypt data in transit and at rest. We do not sell customer content. For customers with strict residency or regulatory needs, we offer Dedicated and Private deployment options. Contact your account team for a region-specific subprocessor snapshot.\"
>
> I recommend the paragraph above; it flags cross-border processing, notes the potential for content-handling (without naming specific vendors in the blurb), and points Procurement to escalation paths.
>
> Kim, can you put together a Console help tooltip (one-line) and a downloadable one-page PDF that matches this text?
>
> Laura Bennett
> Senior Privacy Counsel
> Redwood Inference
> laura.bennett@redwood.com
>

title: Atlas eval: prioritized throughput & p95/p99 action plan
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Priya Menon <priya@atlascloud.com>, Rahul Sharma <rahul@atlascloud.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-22T09:15:00-07:00
Subject: Atlas eval: follow-up from today's call & suggested throughput tests

Hi Priya / Rahul,

Thanks for the time today — helpful to hear your SLOs and the key customer scenarios. Quick recap + a narrow set of tests we recommend to make the hyperscaler comparison defensible: 

Priority summary (from our call):
- Target latency SLO: p95 < 120ms for short chat turns (~64 tokens)
- Long-form background generation: p95 < 800ms for 512-token responses
- Cost sensitivity: prefer tests that reflect tokens/sec and per-token cost at 95th percentile

Proposed focused A/B tests (we’ll run Redwood hosted vs Hyperscaler A API using the same model family):
1) Short-seq single-client: 64-token prompts, 1000 warmup requests, 10k measured requests; measure p50/p95/p99, tokens/sec, tail latency under steady-state.
2) Long-seq burst: 512-token outputs with 20 concurrent connections to emulate parallel UX flows; measure throughput (tokens/sec) and p99 under bursts.
3) Batching sensitivity: run same short-seq at batch sizes [1, 4, 8] to show latency vs cost tradeoffs.
4) KV cache probe: for chat-like flows, measure p95 with and without prefix/KV caching to show savings in tokens/sec and latency.

Deliverables we’ll provide: test harness config, raw CSVs, per-route p50/p95/p99 plots, and a concise recommendation slide deck. Ben will own the lab run; Karthik will validate kernel/quantization parity vs Hyperscaler profiles.

Can you confirm the most realistic production request mix (ratio short:long:multi-turn) and any constraints on example prompts/PII so we can use representative prompts? Also confirm whether you want us to run tests in us-west or your eu region.

Attaching a short eval proposal workbook and the test instructions we plan to execute.

Next steps (proposed):
- Priya: confirm mix and region by EOD Wednesday 8/25
- Redwood (Ben/Karthik): lock test infra and start dry runs on 8/26
- Full A/B runs target week of 8/31

Thanks — happy to tighten scope if you prefer a smaller starter test.

Best,
Ava Chen
Senior Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference

Attachments: atlas-eval-proposal.xlsx, throughput-test-instructions.md


From: Priya Menon <priya@atlascloud.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
Cc: Rahul Sharma <rahul@atlascloud.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-22T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Atlas eval: follow-up from today's call & suggested throughput tests

Thanks Ava — that plan looks good and nicely scoped. A few clarifications from our side: 

1) Request mix: short:long:multi-turn = 60:20:20 (short = < 128 tokens, long = 256-512, multi-turn = 4–6 messages with short turn lengths).
2) Region: run tests in us-west first (we'll consider eu later).
3) Prompts: we will provide a sanitized set of 30 representative prompts (no PII) by end of day Tuesday 8/25.
4) Hyperscaler target: please include Hyperscaler A and Hyperscaler B (their managed LLM endpoints) in the comparison — both are what we currently evaluate.

Important callout: our procurement team will want clear p95/p99 snapshots and cost-per-1000-tokens for each run — the more reproducible the harness the better. Also, if you can include a short section that explains where Redwood's batching + KV cache impacts unit cost versus the Hyperscalers, that will be useful for our CFO.

We’re OK with the proposed schedule. Confirm if Redwood needs an IP or security checklist completed before we hand you the prompts.

Regards,
Priya Menon
Head of Platform
Atlas Cloud


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-23T08:42:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Atlas eval - infra & test notes (internal)

Looping internal notes here so we have an execution checklist. Priya asked for Hyperscaler A & B; we should be explicit about run parity. 

Execution checklist (Ben owner):
- Provision identical VM/GPU shapes in us-west for fairness (instances + network egress as close as possible) — target done by 8/25.
- Use same model weights (open/checksum) and tokenization to avoid model mismatch noise. Where Hyperscaler provides only API, instrument at request/response level to capture latency and token counts.
- Measurement: p50/p95/p99 (latency), tokens/sec, CPU/GPU util, and cost-per-1000-tokens estimated from run usage.
- Batching: implement batch plumbing with sizes [1,4,8] and fixed timeouts to reflect production routing.
- KV cache: two test modes (cold start vs warmed prefix cache). We'll include scripts to preload cache.

Karthik — can you validate that our quantized runtime path produces comparable output to the float baseline for the short test prompts? If the outputs diverge substantively we need to flag quality/regression risk to Priya.

Also: record the exact SDK versions, driver/kernel versions and attach logs so procurement has an audit trail.

Ben
Principal Performance Engineer
Redwood Inference

Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>

- Regions: us-west only (initial)

- Test set: Redwood harness vs Hyperscaler APIs using the same sanitized prompt set you’ll provide

- 8/25 EOD: Atlas shares sanitized prompts (Priya)
- 8/26: Infra provisioned & dry runs (Ben)
- 8/27–8/29: Full A/B runs + KV cache permutations (Ben / Redwood lab)
- 9/01: Initial results slide + raw CSVs delivered to Atlas; cost-per-1k token summary included (Ava)

Deliverables:
- CSVs with request-level latencies, token counts, and resource utilization
- p50/p95/p99 plots and tokens/sec charts for each route

preview:
From: Jordan Matthews <jordan.matthews@orbitpay.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: ops-team@orbitpay.com
Date: 2026-08-22T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Spike in p99 / request timeouts since 08:45 — immediate help needed

Ben —

We started seeing a sharp increase in p95/p99 latency and a handful of timeouts starting ~08:45 PT. Our service health page shows p99 jumping from ~220ms to >2s and a corresponding error-rate bump (5xxs) on the hosted endpoint serving our traffic. Throughput is also lower — we see requests backing up with 429 retries. This started right after your latest rollout this morning (we were on v2026-08-22-03).

Can you confirm whether anything changed on your side (cache settings, model variant routing, prefix invalidation)? This is impacting checkout flows. We need an ETA and mitigation plan.

— Jordan
Director of Product, OrbitPay

---
Attachment: slo-p95-graph.png (image/png)


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Jordan Matthews <jordan.matthews@orbitpay.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-08-22T09:28:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Spike in p99 / request timeouts since 08:45 — immediate help needed

Jordan — thanks for flagging and sorry for the disruption. We see the same symptoms in our dashboards. I'm escalating to our SRE team and product owner immediately.

Quick notes:
- We pushed a small infra config tweak at 08:55 (intended to tighten prefix TTLs for cache churn) but that was targeted at an internal infra shard; we believed it would be isolated.
- I'm CC'ing Marissa (SRE lead) and Sean (Customer Success) to coordinate.

I'll share a short status update in the next 20 minutes with what we find and a temporary mitigation (rollback / bypass) if needed.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
Redwood Inference | ben.carter@redwood.ai


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-08-22T10:05:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Spike in p99 / request timeouts since 08:45 — immediate help needed

Summary of initial triage (SRE):
- Observed p95/p99 regressions starting ~08:46; p99 peak ~2.3s for OrbitPay's key route (/v1/generate).
- Error rate (5xx) correlated with cache-miss amplification: a large set of very similar prefix keys experienced simultaneous eviction around 08:55. We see a burst of KV miss lookups followed by slower cold-path model loads.
- Timeline: config change (infra) rolled at 08:55 that reduced prefix-TTLs from 6h to 30m on the affected pool. There was an unintended broad scope flag — it applied to the hosted pool serving OrbitPay traffic.
- Evidence: attached prefix-eviction-log.txt (excerpt) shows mass evictions and subsequent model load traces with request IDs and latencies. Also linking dashboard: https://console.redwood.ai/dashboards/OrbitPay/p95-heatmaps

Immediate mitigation options we've started:
1) Rollback TTL change to previous value (6h) — being prepared now (impact: quick fix for cache behavior, low-risk).
2) Enable prefix warm-up (proactive KV prefetch for hot prefixes) — longer to fully ramp.
3) Route OrbitPay to a healthy pool (requires routing policy change + ephemeral capacity) — takes ~25 minutes.

We're executing step 1 now. I'll provide confirmation once rollback completes and we'll monitor p95/p99. If the rollback doesn't restore the p99 within 15 minutes we'll trigger 2 and 3.

Marissa Cole
SRE Lead, Redwood Inference
---
Attachment: prefix-eviction-log.txt (text/plain)


From: Jordan Matthews <jordan.matthews@orbitpay.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-08-22T11:40:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Spike in p99 / request timeouts since 08:45 — immediate help needed

Thanks for the quick reply and for starting a rollback. Two asks from our side:
1) OrbitPay legal/ops need a short RCA and confirmation of whether any user data was retried or duplicated (we saw some duplicate tokens generated due to retries).
2) We will need a formal credit request / SLA escalation if the incident duration exceeds 45 minutes. Please loop in someone from leadership if you expect >30 minutes.

Also please confirm whether the rollout change had a changelog/PR and why the scope wasn't constrained to internal shards. We're happy to be on a joint post-mortem.

Jordan
---


From: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-08-22T16:45:00-07:00
Subject: Exec / customer update: mitigation steps and ask for leadership sync

Team —

Thank you for the rapid triage. Status update for orbit pay execs and our leadership:
- Root cause (prelim): unintended broad TTL reduction on prefix cache pool during an infra config rollout at 08:55. This triggered simultaneous prefix eviction which magnified cold-start model loads and led to p99 regressions and transient 5xxs.
- Actions completed:
  * 10:10 — Rolled back TTL to 6h for affected pool (rollback completed, monitoring).
  * 10:25 — Enabled temporary prefix-warmup for top 5 hot prefixes for OrbitPay traffic.
  * 11:00 — Scoped routing rule created; traffic will be moved to alternate pool if we see another spike.
- Current state (16:40): p95 returned to ~280ms, p99 at ~380ms (still elevated vs baseline but much improved). Error-rate back down to baseline.

Customer impact: 40 minutes of elevated p99, several transient timeouts leading to retries. OrbitPay has indicated they'll request SLA credit if >45min. Please preserve all logs and the post-incident timeline for the RCA.

Requests / next steps:
- Sean/GTM: coordinate customer comms and credit process (Sean, please draft a short timeline and proposed remediation credits).
- Maya/Infra: implement a guard to prevent global TTL flag scope changes without a canary/owner approval; propose an emergency rollback runbook.
- Marissa: finish the deep dive and produce an RCA (include why eviction set was larger than expected and whether prefix key hashing caused hot-key collisions).
- Ben: schedule a call with Jordan and their ops lead tomorrow morning for a joint post-mortem.

I'll join the customer call if leadership presence is requested. We should treat this as a priority for the week — it's instructive for our rollout controls and cache safety nets.

Markus Klein
VP, Platform Engineering | Redwood Inference
Phone: +1-415-555-0199

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-08-22T10:05:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Spike in p99 / request timeouts since 08:45 — immediate help needed

Summary of initial triage (SRE):
- Observed p95/p99 regressions starting ~08:46; p99 peak ~2.3s for OrbitPay's key route (/v1/generate).
- Error rate (5xx) correlated with cache-miss amplification: a large set of very similar prefix keys experienced simultaneous eviction around 08:55. We see a burst of KV miss lookups followed by slower cold-path model loads.
- Timeline: config change (infra) rolled at 08:55 that reduced prefix-TTLs from 6h to 30m on the affected pool. There was an unintended broad scope flag — it applied to the hosted pool serving OrbitPay traffic.
- Evidence: attached prefix-eviction-log.txt (excerpt) shows mass evictions and subsequent model load traces with request IDs and latencies. Also linking dashboard: https://console.redwood.ai/dashboards/OrbitPay/p95-heatmaps

Immediate mitigation options we've started:
1) Rollback TTL change to previous value (6h) — being prepared now (impact: quick fix for cache behavior, low-risk).
2) Enable prefix warm-up (proactive KV prefetch for hot prefixes) — longer to fully ramp.
3) Route OrbitPay to a healthy pool (requires routing policy change + ephemeral capacity) — takes ~25 minutes.

We're executing step 1 now. I'll provide confirmation once rollback completes and we'll monitor p95/p99. If the rollback doesn't restore the p99 within 15 minutes we'll trigger 2 and 3.

Marissa Cole
SRE Lead, Redwood Inference
---

Subject: Exec / customer update: mitigation steps and ask for leadership sync

Team —

Thank you for the rapid triage. Status update for orbit pay execs and our leadership:
- Root cause (prelim): unintended broad TTL reduction on prefix cache pool during an infra config rollout at 08:55. This triggered simultaneous prefix eviction which magnified cold-start model loads and led to p99 regressions and transient 5xxs.
- Actions completed:
  * 10:10 — Rolled back TTL to 6h for affected pool (rollback completed, monitoring).
  * 10:25 — Enabled temporary prefix-warmup for top 5 hot prefixes for OrbitPay traffic.
  * 11:00 — Scoped routing rule created; traffic will be moved to alternate pool if we see another spike.
- Current state (16:40): p95 returned to ~280ms, p99 at ~380ms (still elevated vs baseline but much improved). Error-rate back down to baseline.

Customer impact: 40 minutes of elevated p99, several transient timeouts leading to retries. OrbitPay has indicated they'll request SLA credit if >45min. Please preserve all logs and the post-incident timeline for the RCA.

Requests / next steps:
- Sean/GTM: coordinate customer comms and credit process (Sean, please draft a short timeline and proposed remediation credits).
- Maya/Infra: implement a guard to prevent global TTL flag scope changes without a canary/owner approval; propose an emergency rollback runbook.
- Marissa: finish the deep dive and produce an RCA (include why eviction set was larger than expected and whether prefix key hashing caused hot-key collisions).
- Ben: schedule a call with Jordan and their ops lead tomorrow morning for a joint post-mortem.

I'll join the customer call if leadership presence is requested. We should treat this as a priority for the week — it's instructive for our rollout controls and cache safety nets.

Markus Klein
VP, Platform Engineering | Redwood Inference

title: Hosted API rate card & model inventory request - Sentinel Analytics
preview:
From: Sara Lopez <sara@sentinel-analytics.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-22T09:12:00-04:00
Subject: Hosted API rate card & model inventory request

Hi Ben,

Quick intro — I'm Sara, Head of Platform at Sentinel Analytics. We're evaluating hosted inference providers for a new feature (real-time summarization + embeddings) and are compiling baseline pricing to compare unit economics. Can you send your standard per-1M token pricing (separate rates for input vs output if you publish them), and a concise list of the hosted model variants you offer today (name, typical latency tier, recommended use-case)?

High level context:
- Expected pilot: ~5M tokens/month, production target ~20M tokens/month across a mix of summarization and embedding calls.
- Latency: P50 ~150-300ms desirable for the summarization path.
- Compliance: must support SOC 2 and VPC peering as a follow-up conversation, procurement will request standard security docs.

If you have a one-page rate card or short PDF, that would be great. Also include any volume discount thresholds and the typical lead time for a reserved capacity conversation.

Thanks — happy to hop on a 20 minute call if it's easier.

Sara Lopez
Head of Platform | Sentinel Analytics
[sent via personal email]

[This message may be forwarded to procurement for vendor evaluation]

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Sara Lopez <sara@sentinel-analytics.com>
Date: 2026-08-22T11:03:00-04:00
Subject: Re: Hosted API rate card & model inventory request

Sara — thanks for the note and the context, appreciate the specifics.

High level pricing (hosted API, list prices per 1M tokens):
- Base generation (Redwood standard models): $3.50 / 1M input tokens, $5.00 / 1M output tokens
- High-capacity generation (low-latency variants): $6.50 / 1M input, $9.00 / 1M output
- Embeddings (dense vectors): $2.00 / 1M tokens (ingestion and vectorization combined)

Notes:
- All prices are list (self-serve hosted) — we commonly publish input/output separately because inference cost is asymmetric.
- We support streaming and function-calling; billing aligns with tokens consumed by each stream.
- Typical latency tiers: standard models (P50 250–400ms), low-latency variants (P50 100–220ms) — exact numbers depend on sequence length and batch configuration.

Attached is a short overview (Redwood-Hosted-Ratecard-Intro.pdf) with model names and recommended use cases, plus a sample quote template.

If your runway is ~20M tokens/month we often recommend exploring a Reserved / Dedicated capacity conversation (predictable throughput, discounted committed rates). For that we can provide a tailored quote and a 60–90 day lead time for capacity setup if GPUs need to be reserved.

If you can share a rough token mix (percent embeddings vs generation, typical prompt lengths), I can push back a modeled monthly estimate and preliminary discount ranges.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: Redwood-Hosted-Ratecard-Intro.pdf (PDF, 230KB)]

3) Billing cadence: do you support monthly invoicing for committed plans or is it always on usage?

Procurement — looping you in. Contact for contracting is me (sara@sentinel-analytics.com) and procurement@sentinel-analytics.com who will own the PO and terms.

Sara

Subject: Re: Hosted API rate card & model inventory request

Sara — thanks for the note and the context, appreciate the specifics.

High level pricing (hosted API, list prices per 1M tokens):
- Base generation (Redwood standard models): $3.50 / 1M input tokens, $5.00 / 1M output tokens
- High-capacity generation (low-latency variants): $6.50 / 1M input, $9.00 / 1M output
- Embeddings (dense vectors): $2.00 / 1M tokens (ingestion and vectorization combined)

Notes:
- All prices are list (self-serve hosted) — we commonly publish input/output separately because inference cost is asymmetric.
- We support streaming and function-calling; billing aligns with tokens consumed by each stream.
- Typical latency tiers: standard models (P50 250–400ms), low-latency variants (P50 100–220ms) — exact numbers depend on sequence length and batch configuration.

Attached is a short overview (Redwood-Hosted-Ratecard-Intro.pdf) with model names and recommended use cases, plus a sample quote template.

If your runway is ~20M tokens/month we often recommend exploring a Reserved / Dedicated capacity conversation (predictable throughput, discounted committed rates). For that we can provide a tailored quote and a 60–90 day lead time for capacity setup if GPUs need to be reserved.

If you can share a rough token mix (percent embeddings vs generation, typical prompt lengths), I can push back a modeled monthly estimate and preliminary discount ranges.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: Redwood-Hosted-Ratecard-Intro.pdf (PDF, 230KB)]

Subject: Aukera: Evaluation runbook & ownership (next steps)

Hi Samantha —

Thanks again for a productive demo earlier. As promised, below is a concise post-call recap and a proposed runbook for the evaluation so we can get your team into a hands-on trial next week. I’ve mapped out recommended success criteria, an owner for each track, and a tentative timeline. Please review and flag anything missing.

1) Objective
- Validate Redwood Inference for Aukera’s triage assistant workload (latency under 200ms p95, embedding accuracy within current threshold, and predictable per-token cost).

2) Success criteria (proposed)
- Latency: p95 response < 200ms on 512-token prompts.
- Throughput: sustain 30 concurrent sessions during peak window.
- Cost: projected token cost reduction of >= 30% vs current infra on pilot workload.
- Security: confirm SOC2 controls for logs, KMS integration for customer keys, and VPC peering established.
- App integration: demonstration of end-to-end inference calls to your staging environment.

3) Proposed evaluation plan & timeline (2-week pilot)
- Day 0: Kickoff call + account provisioning (this week).
- Days 1–3: Network setup & SSO test (infrastructure team).
- Days 4–10: Run synthetic load + app integration tests.
- Day 11: Joint review of metrics, iterate on config.
- Day 14: Final review & recommendation.

4) Stakeholder / RACI (who owns what) — high level
- Infra (network, VPC, firewall rules, peering): Ben Carter (Redwood) — R / Jordan Lee (Aukera procurement) for procurement coordination — C
- App integration (staging hooks, SDK integration): Marissa Cole (Redwood) — R / Samantha Green (Aukera) — A
- Security & compliance (KMS, logs, evidence): Laura Bennett (Redwood security) — R / Samantha Green — C
- Procurement / contract steps: Aditya Rao (sales) — A / Jordan Lee (Aukera) — R

5) Next steps (actionable)
- Samantha: confirm your procurement contact (we have Jordan on file; please confirm) and whether Aukera requires an executed NDA before sharing network addresses.
- Jordan / Aukera infra: provide IP allowlist or confirm VPC peering window.
- Redwood: I’ll send the Drive folder with the evaluation matrix + network requirements (attached).

title: Blended pricing + PO / billing cadence question
preview:
From: Marcus Lee <mlee@carebridge.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: procurements@carebridge.com
Date: Wed, 19 Aug 2026 16:14:00 -0400
Subject: Blended pricing + PO / billing cadence question

Hi Ben,

Quick procurement question as we finalize the commercial terms: our AP team is pushing for a single PO per calendar quarter rather than multiple POs per invoice run. They also prefer monthly invoices and Net 45 payment terms (we typically run Net 45 for SaaS).

A couple of constraints from our side that matter for discounting:
- We need the PO number to appear in the invoice header (our ERP uses that field for reconciliation).
- If we can commit to a minimum monthly spend (floor), we can qualify for a larger discount band internally.
- We may not be able to add multiple POs per month without an override from our finance manager.

Can Redwood accommodate: 1) monthly invoicing with the PO number in the invoice header, 2) Net 45 terms, and 3) a blended pricing slab that applies the discount based on total quarterly spend rather than per-PO?

If you have a short proposal document or a sample invoice format that shows the PO header field, that'd be super helpful for our AP review.

Thanks,
Marcus
Marcus Lee
Procurement Director | CareBridge Health
mlee@carebridge.com
(415) 555-0192

---
[Attachment: procurement-guidelines-carebridge.pdf (pdf)]

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Marcus Lee <mlee@carebridge.com>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>, Grace Occonnor <grace.occonnor@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 19 Aug 2026 18:02:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Blended pricing + PO / billing cadence question

Hi Marcus — thanks for the clear note and the procurement guidelines. Really appreciate the details.

Short answer: yes, we can support the flows you described but there are a couple of tradeoffs to agree on so we can map discounts in our billing system and keep bookkeeping clean.

Proposal options (high-level):
1) Single quarterly PO (preferred by you): we can accept a single PO for the quarter, issue monthly invoices that reference that PO in the invoice header, and calculate discounts at quarter-end based on aggregated usage. That requires a signed commitment on expected monthly floors so we can apply the larger discount band retroactively or via credit.

2) Per-month PO (if finance insists): our billing can accept one PO per invoice — easiest for automation — but we can still evaluate quarter-to-date spend for discount slabs if you pass us a consolidated PO reference or umbrella PO number.

Notes on terms and timing:
- Net 45 is fine — we'll set the invoice terms to Net 45.
- PO number in header: we will map the PO field to the ERP header (we use a line in our invoice template called \"Customer PO / Ref\").
- Discount mechanics: we typically apply discounts either as an upfront committed credit (pre-funded) or as a retroactive credit applied at quarter close depending on legal preference.

Suggested next steps:
- I attached a draft SOW + pricing exhibit that shows how the invoice header will include the PO.
- Vanessa (cc'd) — can you confirm whether we can do retro credits vs. upfront crediting from a rev ops perspective?
- If CareBridge is comfortable with option 1, we'll produce an invoice sample and a mutual action plan to get legal/finance approvals.

I'll follow up with a one-pager that lays out the two discount flows (retro credit vs. prepay credit) and the observable triggers.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com
(650) 555-0147

[Attachment: SOW_CareBridge_v1.pdf (pdf)]

From: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marcus Lee <mlee@carebridge.com>
Cc: Grace Occonnor <grace.occonnor@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 20 Aug 2026 09:41:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Blended pricing + PO / billing cadence question

Hi Marcus, Ben — looping in a couple quick rev ops points so the procurement ask can be operationalized.

Operational confirmations: 
- We can produce monthly invoices with a distinct \"Customer PO / Ref\" header field and include that value in the PDF as well as the invoice metadata sent to your ERP feed.
- Net 45 will be encoded on the invoice and in our billing metadata; cash application will use the PO string to auto-match where possible.
- For a blended/quarterly discount we prefer to implement as a \"quarterly true-up credit\" rather than a retro active rate change on existing invoices — easier to track in our systems and audit-friendly.

A few asks from our side:
- Please confirm the minimum monthly floor that triggers the 10% slab (e.g., $100k/month or $300k/quarter). We can create a pricing exhibit for the SOW once you confirm the floor.
- If you require a single PO per quarter, confirm whether the PO will be static for the quarter or if you may supply an umbrella PO plus month-specific sub-refs. We need a consistent string to map.

If CareBridge is comfortable with a quarterly true-up credit (applied within 30 days after quarter close), we can include sample journal entries and an invoice sample for AP.

Thanks — happy to hop on a 20-min call if that speeds approvals.

—Vanessa Ortiz
Senior Revenue Operations Manager | Redwood Inference
vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com

[Attachment: pricing-exhibit-v2.xlsx (xlsx)]

From: Marcus Lee <mlee@carebridge.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>
Cc: procurements@carebridge.com, Grace Occonnor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 22 Aug 2026 11:03:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Blended pricing + PO / billing cadence question

Thanks Ben and Vanessa — this looks good and answers the main AP concerns. A few quick clarifications from my CFO's note: 

- Floor: we can commit to a $90k/month minimum for the next two quarters, with the ability to revisit after Q4. That would put us in the mid-tier discount band; we'd need 10% at $100k/month but can accept 8% at $90k/month as an interim.
- PO mechanics: we'll provide an umbrella PO for the quarter (single PO string) and include month-specific reference suffixes (e.g., PO-2026-Q3-001-M08) if you need them. For reconciliation, the umbrella PO is the primary key.
- Credit timing: quarterly true-up credit applied within 30 days of quarter close is acceptable.

Please proceed to share the final SOW with the confirmed pricing exhibit and we'll route it through legal and finance. I'll also include the invoice sample in the AP ticket.

Marcus

From: Marcus Lee <mlee@carebridge.com>

Subject: Blended pricing + PO / billing cadence question

Hi Ben,

Quick procurement question as we finalize the commercial terms: our AP team is pushing for a single PO per calendar quarter rather than multiple POs per invoice run. They also prefer monthly invoices and Net 45 payment terms (we typically run Net 45 for SaaS).

A couple of constraints from our side that matter for discounting:
- We need the PO number to appear in the invoice header (our ERP uses that field for reconciliation).
- If we can commit to a minimum monthly spend (floor), we can qualify for a larger discount band internally.
- We may not be able to add multiple POs per month without an override from our finance manager.

Can Redwood accommodate: 1) monthly invoicing with the PO number in the invoice header, 2) Net 45 terms, and 3) a blended pricing slab that applies the discount based on total quarterly spend rather than per-PO?

If you have a short proposal document or a sample invoice format that shows the PO header field, that'd be super helpful for our AP review.

Thanks,
Marcus

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>

Subject: Re: Blended pricing + PO / billing cadence question

Hi Marcus — thanks for the clear note and the procurement guidelines. Really appreciate the details.

Short answer: yes, we can support the flows you described but there are a couple of tradeoffs to agree on so we can map discounts in our billing system and keep bookkeeping clean.

Proposal options (high-level):
1) Single quarterly PO (preferred by you): we can accept a single PO for the quarter, issue monthly invoices that reference that PO in the invoice header, and calculate discounts at quarter-end based on aggregated usage. That requires a signed commitment on expected monthly floors so we can apply the larger discount band retroactively or via credit.

2) Per-month PO (if finance insists): our billing can accept one PO per invoice — easiest for automation — but we can still evaluate quarter-to-date spend for discount slabs if you pass us a consolidated PO reference or umbrella PO number.

Notes on terms and timing:
- Net 45 is fine — we'll set the invoice terms to Net 45.
- PO number in header: we will map the PO field to the ERP header (we use a line in our invoice template called \"Customer PO / Ref\").
- Discount mechanics: we typically apply discounts either as an upfront committed credit (pre-funded) or as a retroactive credit applied at quarter close depending on legal preference.

Suggested next steps:
- I attached a draft SOW + pricing exhibit that shows how the invoice header will include the PO.
- Vanessa (cc'd) — can you confirm whether we can do retro credits vs. upfront crediting from a rev ops perspective?
- If CareBridge is comfortable with option 1, we'll produce an invoice sample and a mutual action plan to get legal/finance approvals.

I'll follow up with a one-pager that lays out the two discount flows (retro credit vs. prepay credit) and the observable triggers.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com
(650) 555-0147

- Net 45 will be encoded on the invoice and in our billing metadata; cash application will use the PO string to auto-match where possible.
- For a blended/quarterly discount we prefer to implement as a \"quarterly true-up credit\" rather than a retro active rate change on existing invoices — easier to track in our systems and audit-friendly.

A few asks from our side:
- Please confirm the minimum monthly floor that triggers the 10% slab (e.g., $100k/month or $300k/quarter). We can create a pricing exhibit for the SOW once you confirm the floor.
- If you require a single PO per quarter, confirm whether the PO will be static for the quarter or if you may supply an umbrella PO plus month-specific sub-refs. We need a consistent string to map.

If CareBridge is comfortable with a quarterly true-up credit (applied within 30 days after quarter close), we can include sample journal entries and an invoice sample for AP.

Thanks — happy to hop on a 20-min call if that speeds approvals.

—Vanessa Ortiz

title: Exec signoff requested: Strategic Hosted API onboarding incentive (>20%)
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Cc: Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 21 Aug 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Exec signoff requested: Strategic Hosted API onboarding incentive (>20%)

Markus / Evelyn —

Quick one for exec consideration. Veridian Health (clinical SaaS, ~1.2M annual revenue run-rate in pilot) are asking for a deeper-than-normal onboarding credit and a rate concession on Hosted API usage for the first 12 months as part of a strategic marketplace co-marketing and multi-region pilot.

Summary:
- Ask: 27% unit price concession on Hosted API token rates for 12 months, plus $100k onboarding credit applied to first invoice.
- Commitment: 18 month engagement, 12 month minimum commitment to the Billing Tier with a 6-month ramp schedule; expected usage year 1 = 18M tokens/month by month 6.
- Rationale: High strategic value — Veridian is a large referral network; they’ve agreed to co-market a Redwood-hosted integration and list us in their marketplace. Marketing exposure + proof point in health vertical could accelerate similar mid-market deals.
- Risk: Revenue dilution and short-term margin hit; finance estimates ~22% gross margin impact on blended unit economics for year 1 if fully granted.

Attachments: pricing_workbook_v3.xlsx (narrowed scenarios), veridian_mou_draft.pdf, usage_forecast_q3-q4.csv
HubSpot deal: https://app.hubspot.com/deal/9872
Deal team recommendation: Approve up to 28% for 12 months with a mandatory upsell milestone at month 9 (if usage < 60% of forecast, credit is clawed back pro-rata).

Request: need an exec decision today (EOD PST) to keep momentum — they’re asking for an offer to present to their CFO on Monday.

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter | Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0142

From: Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Cc: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 21 Aug 2026 10:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Exec signoff requested: Strategic Hosted API onboarding incentive (>20%)

Ben —
Thanks for the write-up, good summary. A couple quick clarifying points from finance that will affect approval thresholds:
1) Is the $100k onboarding credit refundable / offset if they churn before 6 months? (it currently reads as non-refundable).
2) The forecasted peak usage (18M tokens/mo) — what’s the committed minimum? We need a committed minimum MRR or token floor to model ROI.

Prelim math: at 27% concession our blended token margin drops ~20–24% depending on the caching assumptions in the workbook. If Marketing delivers the co-marketing deliverables by month 3 (we need specific KPIs), I’m comfortable recommending up to 25% short-term. Above that, we need a legal addendum with clawback language and a minimum AMR.

Can you update the MOU draft with a 12-month minimum token floor and a 6-month clawback on onboarding credit? Also flag for legal to review SLA/PII clauses — Veridian will be in scope for HIPAA-adjacent workflows.

—Mina
Mina Haddad | Head of Finance, Redwood Inference
mina.haddad@redwood.com

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>, Legal (contract-review) <legal@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 21 Aug 2026 12:10:00 -07:00
Subject: Recommendation: Approve with conditions — up to 30% for 12 months (structured)

Team —
I reviewed the deal economics and strategic value. My recommendation: approve the concession but structured as follows so we protect unit economics while preserving momentum.

Terms I propose for exec signoff:
- Max concession: 30% off base Hosted API token rate, but applied on a tapering schedule: 30% for months 1–3, 28% for months 4–6, 25% for months 7–12 (weighted blended ≈ 27%).
- Onboarding: $75k credit upfront (not refundable); remaining $25k released only after achieving the month-6 usage milestone (>= 60% of forecast).
- Minimum payment floor: annualized minimum billing = $420k (if actuals fall below, they pay the difference).
- Legal addendum: 6-month clawback on credits, data-security attestation, and co-marketing commitments (deliverables and timelines).
- Review cadence: Finance & CS to re-evaluate at month 6 for renewal / rate reset.

This preserves flexibility (we can advertise a stronger initial concession to the buyer) while ensuring a guardrail for finance. Legal, please start a targeted addendum.

Marcus
Marcus Lin | VP, Sales — Enterprise Accounts
marcus.lin@redwood.com

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>, Legal (contract-review) <legal@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 21 Aug 2026 15:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Exec signoff requested: Strategic Hosted API onboarding incentive (>20%)

Markus / Evelyn —
Looping you both for a quick decision. Options below — we need one selected so I can present a firm offer to Veridian before their CFO meeting Monday.

Options for quick vote:
A) Approve Marcus structure (tapered concession up to 30% with $75k upfront credit + $25k deferred) — legal to finalize addendum.
B) Approve up to 25% (shorter approval, less legal overhead).
C) Decline concession and offer standard onboarding + a co-marketing pilot instead.

If we don’t respond by EOD PST, they’ve indicated they’ll pause negotiations and re-run the RFP.

Thanks — Ben

From: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>, Legal (contract-review) <legal@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 21 Aug 2026 16:42:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Exec signoff requested: Strategic Hosted API onboarding incentive (>20%)

Approved (A) with the following clarifications:
- Approve Marcus’ structured taper up to 30% for the first three months then taper as described.
- $75k onboarding credit up front; $25k contingent on month-6 milestone (>=60% forecast).
- Legal to add explicit clawback language and the minimum payment floor ($420k annualized).
- CS to own the month-6 review and provide usage verification report.

Legal: please draft the addendum today so we can send the offer to Veridian by COB tomorrow.

title: SDK streaming disconnects after long prompt
preview:
From: Maria Gomez <maria@velvethost.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-19T09:14:00-07:00
Subject: SDK streaming disconnects after long prompt

Hey Ben,

Thanks again for the demo this morning — Samir and I were able to exercise the streaming SDK during a long-document summarization flow. We hit an issue when the combined prompt + context exceeded ~2.5k tokens: the client shows a streaming disconnect and the server returns a 502 briefly. Re-attaching minimal repro logs and a short video clip.

Questions: is there a known limit on streaming/session length? Is there a recommended client-side retry pattern we should use to avoid partial outputs? We're evaluating Redwood for a critical feature so any guidance helps.

Attachments: velvet_demo_logs.zip (200KB), screen-clip.mp4 (referenced)

—Maria
Head of Product, VelvetHost


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Maria Gomez <maria@velvethost.com>
Cc: Samir Patel <samir@velvethost.com>
Date: 2026-08-19T11:02:00-07:00
Subject: Re: SDK streaming disconnects after long prompt

Hi Maria — really appreciate the quick repro and video. I'm sorry you ran into that during the demo.

Short answer: we don't have a hard client-side token cap, but we do have operational safeguards at the routing/edge and within the runtime that can abort or retry when memory pressure or unexpected kernel failure occurs. That said, a 502 is not expected for a normal streaming session.

I'm looping in Logan (platform/runtime) and Marissa (sales eng) to get a quick triage and a recommended workaround while your team evaluates. Would you be available for a 30-min troubleshooting call tomorrow at 10:00am PT? If so, we'll attempt a live repro with your payload.

Action: attaching the logs you sent to our internal ticket and will follow up with the runtime team.

Best,
Ben
Senior Sales Engineer, Redwood Inference


From: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-19T12:25:00-07:00
Subject: Re: SDK streaming disconnects after long prompt — internal triage

Ben — thanks for the ping and logs. Quick read: the server-side traceback in velvet_demo_logs.zip points to an OOM-triggered bailout inside the attention kernel when the prefix + generation window hits a particular alignment (long-ish prefix + medium generation). We saw similar failure modes earlier this year but they were mitigated by a runtime patch that tightened buffer checks; looks like this example exercises a rarer alignment.

Immediate suggestions for the call:
- Ask Maria to reproduce with the SDK using the --no-prefetch flag to rule out client prefetch/KV cache growth as a trigger.
- Try trimming user prompt prefix to see if crash correlates with prefix length vs generation length.
- If we can reproduce, we'll run with debug kernels enabled and capture a detailed core/log snapshot.

I'll stand by for the 10am PT repro. If VelvetHost can share a sanitized request payload ahead of time, that will speed things up (we can sign an NDA for that sample if needed).

—Logan
Principal Engineer, Serving Runtime


From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
To: Maria Gomez <maria@velvethost.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-19T12:48:00-07:00
Subject: Re: SDK streaming disconnects after long prompt — next steps

Maria — thanks for agreeing to the call. Confirming 2026-08-20 10:00am PT for the repro session. For the workshop we'll:
- Attempt live repro with your saved payload and the SDK sample you used.
- If the SDK flags (prefetch/caching) matter, I'll try a quick client-side workaround to keep you unblocked for the POC.
- Logan will gather runtime traces if we repro.

Can you upload the sanitized request to the Drive link Ben shared or drop it into the HubSpot file area? If you prefer, we can use a short-term secrets-safe transfer (we can spin up an ephemeral S3 link).

TL;DR: we'll treat this as "field triage" rather than a guaranteed SLA incident; we'll aim to produce a repro and short-term mitigation within 48 hours.

—Marissa
Sales Engineering Lead


From: Maria Gomez <maria@velvethost.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Samir Patel <samir@velvethost.com>
Date: 2026-08-20T10:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: SDK streaming disconnects after long prompt — demo repro call

Thanks team — on the call now and just uploaded the sanitized sample to the Drive link. The file is named repro_payload_sanitized.json. We're ready for a live run.

—Maria


From: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-21T16:02:00-07:00
Subject: Re: SDK streaming disconnects after long prompt — post-triage notes

Quick sync notes from the repro today (internal):
- We reproduced the disconnect with the sanitized payload. Root cause: an off-by-one in buffer sizing for pooled attention buffers when prefix lengths align on a 512-token boundary with a mid-sized generation window. This leads to a transient write past end-of-buffer and the runtime triggers a controlled 502 abort.
- Short-term mitigation (deployed as a hotfix to the staging cluster during call): runtime now pads pooled buffers by +1 allocation for the affected kernels. This reduces throughput by ~0.6% but prevents the crash. Patch id: RT-HF-2026-0819.
- Recommended SDK guidance for customers until official release: set --no-prefetch or limit prefix to <2300 tokens when doing long prompt + generation mixes; enable client-side single-shot retry with idempotency token to resume safely. We'll document this in the KB article.
- Next steps: create an internal RFC to make padding permanent with a perf-cost analysis, and include a test case in the CI repro matrix for long-prefix alignments. Logan to own RFC; Marissa to draft customer-facing KB; Ben to update HubSpot deal notes.

Attachments included for the team: repro_steps.txt, screenshot-rt-disconnect.png

—Sean
Staff Sales Engineer (Operations Liaison)
